description.task: Simulate different Spring Boot test slices. Create test classes that represent @WebMvcTest (controller only), @DataJpaTest (repository only), and @SpringBootTest (full context).
instructions: FULL_SPRING_BOOT case: add all 8 beans from both cases plus 'UserService' and 'SecurityFilter'.
description.hint: @WebMvcTest loads only web layer — faster tests. @DataJpaTest loads only data layer with in-memory H2. @SpringBootTest loads everything — use sparingly as it's slow.
description.expectedOutput:
=== Test Context: WEB_MVC ===
Loaded beans (4): [ApplicationContext, DispatcherServlet, UserController, MockMvc]
Has Controller: true
Has Repository: false
Has Service: false

=== Test Context: DATA_JPA ===
Loaded beans (4): [ApplicationContext, EntityManager, UserRepository, DataSource]
Has Controller: false
Has Repository: true
Has Service: false

=== Test Context: FULL_SPRING_BOOT ===
Loaded beans (9): [ApplicationContext, DispatcherServlet, UserController, MockMvc, EntityManager, UserRepository, DataSource, UserService, SecurityFilter]
Has Controller: true
Has Repository: true
Has Service: true

description.task: Simulate Spring MockMvc by building a MockHttpClient that performs GET and POST requests to controller methods and captures the HTTP response for assertions.
instructions: Run Main as-is — the implementation is complete.
Trace each test: GET /todos returns status 200 + JSON; POST /todos creates; GET /invalid returns 404.
description.expectedOutput:
Test: GET /todos
GET /todos
  Status 200: PASS
  Body contains 'Learn Spring': PASS
  Content-Type 'application/json': PASS
Test: POST /todos
POST /todos body=Read Clean Code
  Status 201: PASS
  Body contains 'Created': PASS
Test: GET /invalid
GET /invalid
  Status 404: PASS

description.task: Implement the Builder pattern for creating test objects. Write a ProductTestDataBuilder that creates valid Product objects for testing with sensible defaults that can be overridden.
description.context: Test data builders reduce test setup boilerplate and make tests more readable. Also known as the Object Mother pattern when combined with factory methods.
instructions: Add fluent setter methods to Builder: id(int), name(String), category(String), price(double), stockCount(int), active(boolean), tags(List<String>). Each returns 'this'.
All three products should display correctly.
description.hint: Test builders make test setup expressive: new Product.Builder().withName('Test').withPrice(50).outOfStock().inactive().build(). Static factory methods like Product.aDefaultProduct() improve readability further.
